column1: You already know several methods for solving quadratic equations, such as factoring, completing the square, and using the Quadratic Formula. Mathematicians have developed formulas for solving third- and fourth-degree polynomial equations, but these formulas are far more complicated and messy than the Quadratic Formula is, and they are rarely used. Furthermore, for polynomials of degree greater than four, there is no single formula to use. For many polynomials, you can develop more useful methods than a formula based on what you already know.

column1: Example: Suppose the integers a, b,c, and d are zeros of a polynomial. Then, according to the Factor Theorem, (x — a)(x — b)(x-—.c)(x-—d) are factors of the polynomial.
